name: Narcolepsy and Cataplexy
description: Canine narcolepsy is a sleep disorder characterised by sudden bouts of sleep or cataplexy (sudden muscle paralysis triggered by excitement). It is caused by a deficiency in orexin (hypocretin) signalling and can be hereditary or sporadic.
symptoms: Cataplexy triggered by eating or excitement
treatment_overview: Imipramine or dextroamphetamine reduce cataplectic attacks. Many dogs with mild disease require no treatment. Triggers should be minimised. The condition does not shorten lifespan.
prevention_tips: DNA test available for familial forms in Dobermanns and Labradors
